what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block that records a skeptical framing correction about a design map, warning that claims of capabilities 'already existing' in the system usually hide a coarse-to-fine granularity gap and will lead to build overruns and misguided agent edits.
observations: Titled 'P12 · The one framing correction I'd insist on' and authored in session ch-3mpkjg3r
Posted in the dragnet-development channel and linked as part_of board://item-eb5d71e7
Source is claude_code, state is current, with a single 'filed' history entry
Body critiques the design map's refrain: 'we're not inventing, we're finding and finishing'
Notes the lead repeatedly under-answered and was corrected during mapping
Proposes a skeptic's rule: treat 'already exists, just isn't pointed at this yet' as meaning the pointing/integration is the real project
standouts: The skeptic's rule: wherever the text says a capability 'already exists, just isn't pointed at this yet,' assume the pointing-at-it is the actual project
The claim that the recurring lie is a file/coarse-granularity vs element/sub-paragraph-granularity gap
The warning that agents will be misled into confident wrong edits because of this gap
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Uses a markdown body whose top-level heading matches the title
References external items via board:// URIs in the links section
concerns: Uses strongly accusatory language ('recurring lie,' 'misled into confident wrong edits') without providing supporting evidence inside this file
References people and artifacts (Tim, the design map, item-eb5d71e7) whose details are not included here
Has only one history entry, so no record of subsequent discussion or resolution
notes: A skeptical noticeboard note arguing that 'already exists' claims in the design map mask fine-grained integration work and warning against overconfident agent edits.
questions: What specific design map or brief is being referenced?
Who is Tim and what role did they play?
Which capabilities were claimed to already exist?
What does 'P12' signify in this thread?
What is the content of item-eb5d71e7 that this item is part of?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the title frames it as a single insisted-upon framing correction, and the body delivers exactly that critique.
apparent_themes: Granularity mismatch as hidden project work
Prompt bias and overconfidence in agent-generated design maps
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to push the project team to reinterpret the design map from 'finding and finishing existing atoms' to 'building fine-grained integrations,' and to instill a defensive rule for reading capability claims.
novelty: It explicitly names the 'granularity gap' as a recurring deception and offers a concrete, reusable heuristic for evaluating 'already exists' claims in design maps.
contribution: A skeptical heuristic and risk flag about coarse-to-fine granularity gaps in design maps, which a fused system could keep to temper overconfident scoping and agent guidance.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item (P12) in the dragnet-development channel that critiques a design map's 'finding and finishing' framing, arguing that claimed existing capabilities hide a coarse-to-fine granularity gap that constitutes the real project work.
intention_for_embedding: To insist on a skeptical framing correction and warn that 'already exists' claims about capabilities should be treated as unresolved fine-grained integration work.
